feedback.text: I would like to add on to my previous statement, and argue that OEM manufacturers of phones such as Samsung and Motorola should be required to allow the bootloaders of their devices to be unlocked. The EU law requiring only official software to run on phones needs to be pulled back as it violates consumer freedom and stifles competition in the operating system market. Regardless of the risk, so long as I know what I am doing and have informed consent, it should be my right to install whatever alternate operating system I wish to on my Android phone. Laws like this only ensure Samsung and Motorola obtain a monopoly on the operating system market, as users cannot use alternate operating systems such as LineageOS, PixelOS, and other open source competitors, many of which respect user privacy much more than the operating systems provided by Samsung and Motorola. If Samsung believes it's operating system is more secure than something like LineageOS, or provides better services via their Samsung Ecosystem, then users should be given the choice to decide themselves. Unlocking a bootloader is not a simple task. You have to go out of your way to research how to do so, go through several complicated steps involving multiple security warnings, and have knowledge of how to load the Android ROM itself onto the phone. A person does not accidentally accidentally unlock their bootloader, let alone install a malicious operating system on their phone. If a user is willing to go through these complicated steps, then they most likely know what they are doing, and if they do not then they are most certainly accepting any risks and potential threat to their security. Companies like Google, Samsung, Motorola, and others should not be responsible for keeping their users safe, nor restricting their freedoms in the name of this safety. I would much rather trust the various governments of the EU and other democratic nations, who are directly accountable to the public and far more transparent, to protect me from any malicious apps via legal action directly. I implore the EU to require the option to unlock phone bootloaders to by law be present on any phone manufacturer's products.
